steps.specialNote: نَبِيّ (singular) → أَنبِيَاء (plural, broken). This is a broken plural — the internal vowel pattern changes completely. Broken plurals are one of Arabic's most distinctive features.

ayahCards.arabicText: وَمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْبَاطِ وَمَا أُوتِيَ مُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَمَا أُوتِيَ النَّبِيُّونَ
ayahCards.translation: And what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob…and what was given to Moses and Jesus, and what was given to the prophets.

steps.specialNote: رَسُول (singular) → رُسُل (plural, broken). Notice how the vowels shift internally. رَسُول is one of the most frequent Isms in the Quran.

ayahCards.translation: Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah.

steps.question: The ـُونَ ending on مُسْلِمُونَ indicates what?
steps.explanation: ـُونَ (nominative) or ـِينَ (accusative/genitive) marks the sound masculine plural — the plural pattern where the base word stays intact and only the ending changes.
